runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_coordinate_policy: Coordinate system: x and y MUST be fractions in the closed range [0.0, 1.0] relative to the map image. Origin (0,0) is the top-left corner of the image; (1,1) is the bottom-right corner. Do NOT return pixel coordinates, percentages (0-100), or any other unit. Example: a node at the horizontal center and 3/4 down the image is x=0.5, y=0.75. Always include at most 3 decimal places.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_dedup_policy: Deduplicate nodes by semantic identity. Do not assume any organization-specific naming convention. Keep one canonical node when items represent the same physical bin/buffer/location, even if IDs differ. Prefer IDs explicitly present on maps/tables; otherwise normalize ID from label. Merge metadata fields into the canonical node.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_exclusion_policy: Exclude canceled/inactive/decommissioned/removed/closed trash bins or buffer points. Do not output such items as nodes even if present in source artifacts.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_support_joint_prompt: Extract all bin/buffer node metadata by jointly analyzing the map image and support artifact image. Be exhaustive and preserve IDs exactly as they appear.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_support_fallback_prompt: Extract all bin/buffer node metadata from the provided support artifact image. Be exhaustive and preserve IDs exactly as they appear.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_support_delta_prompt: Extract any additional bin metadata missed previously from the support artifact image.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_normalize_prompt: Normalize the provided extracted content into JSON nodes.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_normalize_context: Produce JSON only with shape {"nodes": [...]} where each node includes id/label/type/metadata when available. Keep IDs exactly as seen in source text. SUPPORT CSV / EXTRACTED TEXT: <follow by concatenated string of all output from state 3 files>
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_support_delta_context: Return concise text only. Focus on IDs and attributes not already extracted.
runtime_prompts.compact_output_policy: Minimize output tokens: return only required fields, no explanations, no duplicated rows, no markdown wrappers unless explicitly requested.
runtime_prompts.tabular_extraction_output_hint:
requirement
- Output the extracted data in CSV format, ensuring all column headers and row alignments perfectly match the visual table.
- Return CSV plain text only, with a header row.
- fill data base on prior table given below
- aware of semantic note in data and make decision focus on recording current system behaviour; canceled should not be recorded as current state.
runtime_prompts.tabular_extraction_no_support_hint: No support table image was uploaded; extract any visible tabular metadata from the map image. MAP OCR TEXT CONTEXT (used when support image is unavailable):
runtime_prompts.extractmap_symbol_output_hint: Return JSON only with shape {"symbols":[{"symbol":"string","notation":"string","description":"string","color":"string"}]}. Keep symbol values short, uppercase, and enum-like (e.g., RED, GREEN, BLUE, HAZARDOUS). Process only the provided single map image.
runtime_prompts.extractmap_text_symbol_usage_policy: Use the provided symbol legend as canonical enum source. For each node, populate metadata.symbols as an array of enum strings. If a node has multiple symbols, include all of them in metadata.symbols in deterministic order. Do not invent symbols outside the provided enum list unless unavoidable; use UNKNOWN as fallback.
runtime_prompts.edge_extraction_dedup_policy: Deduplicate edges by topology. If multiple rows describe the same traversal link, keep one canonical edge with the best available cost/label. Avoid source-target duplicates regardless of ID formatting.
runtime_prompts.edge_extraction_csv_fallback_hint: Return JSON only. Do not return CSV fallback.

extractmap_symbol.prompt: Analyze the provided map image and extract map legend symbols with notation and meaning. Output strictly as JSON for downstream processing.

extractmap_text.task_description: Extract node metadata and update existing JSON.
extractmap_text.note: Process only one image at a time to ensure high extraction accuracy.
extractmap_text.prompt: Analyze the provided map image and extract all text and symbols located near the 'buffer' labels. Cross-reference this with the provided buffer trait table. Update the provided incomplete JSON file with this new data. Categorize each item with a node type label (e.g., 'building', 'indoor_buffer', 'outdoor_buffer'). Output the final, completed data strictly in JSON format.

tabular_extraction.task_description: Convert visual tables into structured data.
tabular_extraction.prompt: Extract all data from the provided table image.

edge_extraction.task_description: Build a traversal graph with estimated edge costs.
edge_extraction.prompt: Using the provided map image and the reference node data from the JSON file, identify all possible traversal paths between nodes. Estimate traversal cost based on visual scale. Return JSON only using the configured edge schema.
